consequence.description: Parallel bumping forces uniform shifts across all tenors, which contradicts how traders conceptualize curve movements and produces inconsistent risk measures that don't align with market practice

core.when: When implementing numerical methods for equity volatility surface construction
core.action: Use Newton-Secant hybrid root finding for delta-to-strike conversion — combine Newton's quadratic convergence near the root with Secant's robustness when derivative information is unreliable

consequence.description: Using pure bisection for delta-to-strike conversion causes excessive iteration counts, significantly slowing vol surface construction; using pure Newton may diverge without good starting values, leading to failed strikes and incomplete vol grids

core.action: Verify the interpolation method matches the curve usage context — PCHIP (BD-037) is for log-discount fitting only, while bootstrap-suitable curves (BD-040) require flat forward method; do not use a single method for both fitting and bootstrap without explicit switching logic

consequence.description: Using PCHIP with bootstrap-suitable curves causes numerical failures since PCHIP cannot guarantee positive forward rates required for bootstrapping; curve construction workflows may silently switch to incorrect interpolation, distorting forward rate calculations across the entire curve

core.action: Use FORDOM (Foreign/Domestic) convention for FXVanillaOption — Foreign currency is base for notional, Domestic for payoff; verify consistent delta hedging direction across each FX option valuations

core.action: Use Hagan 2002 SABR lognormal volatility approximation formula — the approximation provides closed-form implied vol from SABR parameters enabling fast calibration to market swaption vols and arbitrage-free extrapolation
